jsonLdBlocks.headline: Kitchen Remodel Cost Guide
jsonLdBlocks.description: Complete kitchen remodel cost guide for 2026. Compare prices for minor, mid-range, and high-end kitchen renovations and learn how to get the best value.

mainEntity.name: How much does a kitchen remodel cost in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: A mid-range kitchen remodel costs $30,000-$65,000 for a standard-sized kitchen. Minor refreshes start around $8,000, while high-end renovations with custom cabinets and premium appliances can exceed $150,000.

mainEntity.name: What is the most expensive part of a kitchen remodel?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cabinets are typically the largest expense, accounting for 25-35% of the total budget. Custom cabinetry can easily cost $25,000-$40,000 for a mid-sized kitchen.

mainEntity.name: How long does a kitchen remodel take?

acceptedAnswer.text: A minor refresh takes 2-4 weeks. A mid-range remodel typically runs 6-10 weeks. A full gut renovation with structural changes can take 12-16 weeks or longer.

mainEntity.name: Does a kitchen remodel increase home value?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. A mid-range kitchen remodel recovers roughly 60-75% of its cost at resale, and a minor refresh can recover 80% or more.

mainEntity.name: Should I remodel my kitchen before selling?

acceptedAnswer.text: If your kitchen is seriously outdated (1980s or 1990s style), a minor to mid-range update is usually worth the investment. If the kitchen was updated in the last 10-15 years, cosmetic touches may be sufficient.

mainEntity.name: Can I remodel a kitchen for under $20,000?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but you will need to keep the existing layout and focus on cosmetic updates. Painting or refacing cabinets, installing laminate countertops, updating lighting and hardware, and adding a new backsplash can transform a kitchen for $10,000-$18,000.
